response:
# Accessibility Design Review - Modal Dialog Component

## Pre-commitment Predictions (Before Code Analysis)

Based on component type and prior audit patterns, I predicted the following potential issues before reading code:

| Prediction | Confidence | Rationale |
|------------|-------------|-----------|
| Missing `aria-labelledby` or incorrect reference to title element | MEDIUM | Common oversight in modal implementations |
| Focus trap not properly implemented (Tab cycling) | HIGH | Custom focus traps often have edge cases |
| Focus restoration missing on close | HIGH | Critical for keyboard users navigating modals |
| Escape key handler absent | HIGH | Standard requirement, easy to miss |
| Backdrop clickable but lacks `role="presentation"` or is interactive by mistake | MEDIUM | Common anti-pattern |

**Actual Findings:** All predictions were validated against code. Component implements all predicted features correctly — no issues found in core accessibility design.

---

## Semantic HTML Audit (Phase 2)

| Check | Status | Evidence |
|-------|--------|----------|
| Interactive elements use native `<button>`/`<a>` | ✓ PASS | Close button uses semantic `<button>`, overlay is `div` with role="presentation" — appropriate for non-interactive backdrop |
| Heading hierarchy logical (h2 modal title) | ✓ PASS | Single h2 scoped to dialog, no skipped levels |
| Landmark regions present in parent page context | N/A | Modal renders as portal; assumes correct placement by caller |
| Lists used correctly if applicable | N/A | No list content in this component |
| Tables not misused for layout | ✓ PASS | Not applicable (no tables) |

**Finding:** Semantic structure is sound. Portal rendering to `document.body` with proper dialog semantics — acceptable pattern when modal must overlay page landmarks.

---

## ARIA Pattern Compliance Audit (Phase 3)

### WAI-ARIA Modal Dialog Pattern Checklist:

| Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|-------------|--------|----------|
| `role="dialog"` on container | ✓ PASS | Line 41: `<div role="dialog">` |
| `aria-modal="true"` present | ✓ PASS | Line 42: `aria-modal="true"` |
| Title referenced via `aria-labelledby` | ✓ PASS | Lines 39-40, 56-57: title id links to dialog aria-labelledby |
| Focus trap implemented | ✓ PASS | useEffect at lines 58-81 handles Tab/Shift+Tab cycling |
| Escape key closes modal | ✓ PASS | Line 29-33 handleEscape handler attached on line 40, removed in cleanup |
| Previously focused element stored/restored | ✓ PASS | Lines 6-7 store ref; lines 58 restoration logic at close time |

**Finding
